facts.q: QuantConnect / LEAN for NSE India options in 2025-26: Does QC provide NSE/India options, index, or futures…
facts.a: QuantConnect (QC) provides NO native NSE/India options, index-options, or F&O futures DATA for backtesting. Its only India dataset is TrueData "India Equities" — ~2,053 NSE cash equities, daily/minute resolution, from Jan 2010, plus an India Equity Security Master; there is no India options or futures data product despite TrueData itself being an authorized NSE F&O vendor. QC supports two Indian brokerages for LIVE trading — Zerodha (Kite Connect) and Samco — both available on QC Cloud and via LEAN CLI/local. Critically, BOTH plugins are EQUITY/COMMODITY ONLY: they route to NSE/BSE EQUITY and MCX COMMODITY segments with product types MIS/CNC/NRML, and neither plugin's docs mention options or F&O — so no native F&O order routing. For backtesting NSE options you must ingest CUSTOM data, but here the architecture bites: LEAN's native OptionChain, Greeks/IV pricing models (BlackScholes, BinomialCoxRossRubinstein, ForwardTree), and the standard option order/fill pipeline are wired to QC's native Option SecurityType. Custom-imported option records are NOT registered as Option securities, so AddOptionContract throws KeyErrors, contracts can't be ordered, and Greeks aren't auto-computed; QC staff (Louis Szeto) only offered partial workarounds (Symbol.CreateOption to mint symbols, or treating each contract as a separate custom-data class) and did not confirm native Greeks/fill treatment. Realistic workarounds: (1) import custom NSE options data as a custom type, compute Greeks/IV yourself (e.g. QuantLib/py_vollib) and run delta-hedging logic in code, attaching a custom FeeModel (subclass FeeModel.GetOrderFee) to model Rs 20/order brokerage + STT (0.1% of premium on sell side since Oct 1 2024, up from 0.0625%) + exchange/GST/stamp charges, plus a custom FillModel/SlippageModel — but you lose the turnkey OptionChain/Greeks engine; (2) run QC purely for signal/research and execute via an external broker API (Kite Connect, etc.) outside LEAN. The cleanest path that keeps LEAN's full options stack (native chains, Greeks, IV, fee/fill models, code-driven delta hedging) is to use QC-NATIVE US options instead: AlgoSeek US Index Options (SPX/SPXW, VIX/VIXW, NDX, RUT — European style, minute/hourly/daily, from Jan 2012) and US Equity Options (~4,000 underlyings incl. SPY, minute, from Jan 2012), with a daily pre-computed Greeks/IV universe also available. Net: for an NSE long-vol strategy, QC gives you a great engine but no Indian options data and no F&O execution — you either fully roll your own custom-data layer or pivot the research to US SPX/SPY/VIX where everything works out-of-the-box.

facts.q: Empirical shape of the NIFTY/BANKNIFTY implied-vol surface and event behaviour: index put-skew steepness…
facts.a: The NIFTY/BANKNIFTY surface shows the textbook equity-index signature: a steep NEGATIVE (reverse) put skew where OTM puts carry materially higher IV than equidistant calls — a 25-delta put-call skew (risk reversal) of roughly +3 to +6 vol points in normal conditions, steepening sharply (puts bid hard) in stress, driven by structural downside-hedging demand and the index's leverage/correlation effect. Single-stock skews are reliably SHALLOWER/flatter than the index skew (the classic index-vs-single-name asymmetry: idiosyncratic-correlation in the index makes index downside vol richer than the average constituent). Term structure is normally upward-sloping (contango) — the long-run analogue to VIX being in contango ~80-84% of the time — and INVERTS into backwardation during stress/crashes (short-dated IV spikes above long-dated), as seen in COVID-Mar-2020 and the Jun-4-2024 election shock. Event behaviour: India VIX/NIFTY IV reliably CRUSHES on resolution of scheduled binary events. On Union Budget day, a 15-year study (2011-2025) shows India VIX fell on all 15 budget days by an average of more than ~9% (e.g. Feb-1-2025 budget: VIX -12.21% to 14.26 with NIFTY essentially flat) — short premium/straddles tend to beat long straddles on budget day. RBI policy days are a milder, less consistent crush: VIX typically bleeds 5-12% over the run-up as the decision is largely anticipated, but the announcement-day move is mixed and can even tick UP if the outcome/commentary surprises (e.g. +4.83% to 21.43 after the Apr-9-2025 cut; +2.67% to 16.60 after the Aug-2024 hold). General ELECTIONS are the opposite — they INFLATE IV and pay LONG vol through the event because the outcome is a genuine fat-tail: into the 2024 Lok Sabha vote India VIX ran from a 52-week low near 8.4 / mid-teens up ~80-191% to ~24-26 by late May; it dipped on the Jun-3 exit-poll relief rally (VIX -22% to ~19.2 as NIFTY +3.25%) and then EXPLODED on the Jun-4 result-day surprise — VIX +27.7% close to 26.74 (intraday ~31, some sources cite a ~51% intra-event spike) while NIFTY crashed ~6% (-1,379 pts), a textbook long-vol payoff for anyone holding gamma through the event. Single stocks show a clear pre-earnings IV RAMP and post-earnings crush: Indian IT names (TCS/Infosys) ramp ATM IV to ~32-44% into results then crush 35-50% within 24h; the implied move (~3-5%) usually exceeds the realized move (~2-4%), so naively buying-and-holding straddles through earnings tends to lose. Weekly expiry-day gamma dynamics: short-dated/0DTE NIFTY weeklies are the most gamma-dense instruments, so dealer positioning drives pinning to high-OI strikes when dealers are long gamma (stabilizing, mean-reverting) and accelerates/whips moves when short gamma; the stabilizing influence vanishes overnight as 0DTE expires. CRITICAL REGIME CHANGE: BANKNIFTY weekly options were discontinued (last weekly expiry Nov-13-2024; only monthly BANKNIFTY now, expiring last Tuesday), so weekly expiry-gamma analysis now applies to NIFTY only, and NIFTY's weekly expiry moved from Thursday to TUESDAY effective Sep-1-2025.

facts.q: All-in cost stack for retail NSE option buyer 2025-26; round-trip cost percent for ATM and far-OTM; did STT…
facts.a:
For 2025-26 (rates effective 1 Oct 2024 to 31 Mar 2026), all levies are charged on the option premium. STT is 0.10% on sell-side premium at square-off, or 0.125% of intrinsic value on ITM exercise/settlement (cash-settled index options are deemed exercised). NSE exchange transaction charge is 0.03553% of premium per side (about Rs 3,503/crore). SEBI turnover fee is 0.0001% (Rs 10/crore) per side. GST is 18% on brokerage plus exchange charge plus SEBI fee, not on STT or stamp duty. Stamp duty is 0.003% (Rs 300/crore) on the buy side only. Brokerage at discount brokers (Zerodha, Groww, Upstox) is flat Rs 20 per executed order, Rs 40 round trip. Round-trip cost (square-off, NIFTY lot 75): (a) a liquid ATM weekly at Rs 100/unit (Rs 7,500/lot) costs about Rs 61, roughly 0.8% of premium, dominated by flat brokerage plus GST since percentage levies are tiny; (b) a cheap far-OTM at Rs 2/unit (Rs 150/lot) costs about Rs 47, roughly 30%+ of premium, because the flat Rs 40 brokerage plus about Rs 7 GST swamps it (about 13% at Rs 5/unit). The flat per-order brokerage, not the taxes, punishes cheap tails; mitigated by more lots per order. STT did NOT triple in 2024: options sell-side STT rose 0.0625% to 0.10% effective 1 Oct 2024, about a 60% increase (futures likewise 0.0125% to 0.02%). Outside this window, from 1 Apr 2026 options STT rose to 0.15% sell and 0.15% exercise per Budget 2026-27.</answer>
</invoke>

facts.q: India VIX tradability and construction; can retail get direct long vol via any India VIX product today or…
facts.a: India VIX launched April 2008 under a CBOE methodology license, a model free index from OTM near and next month NIFTY 50 options on a 30 day horizon, using NSE MIBOR as risk free rate and NIFTY futures as forward. NSE listed FUTURES from 26 Feb 2014 but liquidity collapsed to a reported single transaction in FY2016 to 17, last traded about Feb 2016, so it is de facto dead. No India VIX options or ETFs or ETNs were ever listed. So retail CANNOT get direct long vol via any India VIX product, and long vol must come from NIFTY or stock options. India VIX still works fully as a regime and cheapness GAUGE off the liquid NIFTY options book, but is not itself tradable.

facts.q: NSE F&O structure as of late-2025/2026: current lot sizes and approximate rupee notional for NIFTY,…
facts.a: Lot sizes moved in two phases. SEBI's Oct-2024 circular raised the minimum index-derivative contract value from ~Rs 5 lakh to Rs 15 lakh (lot sized so notional sits in the Rs 15-20 lakh band on the review day), effective 20 Nov 2024, which roughly tripled lots: NIFTY 25->75, BANKNIFTY 15->30 (later 35), FINNIFTY 25->65, MIDCPNIFTY 50->120 (later 140), NIFTYNEXT50 10->25. NSE then RE-BASELINED lots downward for the January-2026 series (weeklies from the first Jan-2026 expiry; quarterly/half-yearly transitioned 30 Dec 2025; monthlies from the Jan month-end): NIFTY 75->65, BANKNIFTY 35->30, FINNIFTY 65->60, MIDCPNIFTY 140->120 (NIFTYNEXT50 unchanged at 25). At early/mid-2026 index levels the approximate per-lot notional is roughly: NIFTY (~25,000 x 65) about Rs 16-17 lakh; BANKNIFTY (~57,000 x 30) about Rs 17 lakh; FINNIFTY (~27,000 x 60) about Rs 16 lakh; MIDCPNIFTY (~13,500 x 120) about Rs 16 lakh — all within SEBI's ~Rs 15-20 lakh design band (exact rupee figures move with spot). On WEEKLY expiries, SEBI's "one weekly per exchange" rule (effective 20 Nov 2024) leaves NSE with weekly options on NIFTY 50 only (BANKNIFTY, FINNIFTY/Nifty Financial Services and MIDCPNIFTY weeklies were discontinued; those now trade monthly only), and BSE with weekly options on SENSEX only (BANKEX/Sensex-50 weeklies dropped). On the expiry DAY: NSE shifted NIFTY weekly expiry from Thursday to TUESDAY effective 1 Sep 2025; BSE moved SENSEX to Thursday the same day. (Note: NSE had earlier proposed a MONDAY expiry from 5 Apr 2025 but deferred/scrapped it after SEBI's consultation paper standardised expiries to Tuesday or Thursday — so Monday never took effect; the answer is Tuesday for NSE.) Single-stock F&O count is roughly 220-230 securities (after +45 added 29 Nov 2024, with periodic exclusions: 16 removed effective Feb-2025, 4 more dropped after the Dec-2025 expiry, +6 added 1 Apr 2026) — the exact live number changes quarterly and is best confirmed on nseindia.com. Settlement: index options are CASH-settled; all single-stock futures and options are PHYSICALLY settled (compulsory delivery of the underlying) since the phased rollout completed by the Oct-2019 expiry, with net cash+F&O settlement on expiry from the Mar-2023 cycle. The Do-Not-Exercise (DNE) facility (lets a holder instruct the broker not to exercise to avoid physical delivery on close-to-money/ITM stock options) was discontinued Oct 2021 and REINTRODUCED 28 Apr 2022; it remains available, limited to a narrow band (about 3 strikes / CTM contracts) of stock options and does not apply to cash-settled index options.

facts.q: India variance/volatility risk premium: Is NIFTY implied volatility (India VIX / NIFTY ATM IV) systematically…
facts.a: Yes. NIFTY implied volatility is systematically above subsequent realized volatility, i.e. India runs a positive volatility/variance risk premium (VRP), so option sellers are paid on average. The best recent measurement (Nifty 50, ATM IV via Black-76 vs Yang-Zhang realized vol, Aug 2022-Mar 2026, ~887 trading days, 43M+ one-minute bars) finds the premium positive on ~74.9% of days with a mean of about +1.2 vol points; this is corroborated by broker/practitioner sources citing "VRP positive ~74% of the time." India's average vol-point premium is notably SMALLER than the US: over 1990-2024 the VIX averaged 19.59% vs 15.50% realized S&P 500 vol, a ~4.1 vol-point gap (CFA Institute). The premium is regime-dependent and NOT a constant cushion: it inverts (realized exceeds implied, VRP goes negative) roughly a quarter of the time in India (~25.1% inversion rate) and during acute stress. In the US the average IV-RV spread was positive every year since 1990 except 2008, and during the onset of crises (2008 GFC, 2020 COVID) realized vol spiked above implied before the premium re-widened — so the premium narrows/inverts going INTO a shock and is largest in the calm/recovery phase. Academic work confirms variance risk is priced in India: long synthetic variance swaps and market-neutral NIFTY straddles earn statistically significant negative returns (Indian Review of Economics & Finance / Kapadia-style evidence). NSE single-stock options carry a related but distinct premium: Chakrabarti (2021, Applied Economics Letters) shows the single-stock-option VRP co-moves strongly ("commonality") with the index VRP, but individual-name VRPs are generally smaller/noisier than the index because index options embed a correlation/systematic-risk premium that single names lack — so the index is the cleaner, larger premium harvest. On mean reversion: India VIX is strongly mean-reverting around a long-run level commonly cited in the high-teens (~15-20; the full-sample arithmetic mean since 2008 is dragged toward ~18-20 by the 2008 and 2020 spikes, while the post-2014 "normal" regime clusters ~13-17). The VRP series itself shows AR(1) ≈ 0.79 (daily), implying a half-life of roughly 3 trading days; India VIX level mean-reversion is slower than the premium but still fast, with spikes typically decaying back toward the mean within weeks.
